text: An eighty-eight year old female presented with symptomatic dysphagia from a large Zenker's diverticulum for flexible endoscopic incisional therapy.
The surgeon performs the procedure using a flexible endoscope equipped with a distal cap attachment and an insulated tip needle knife, applying endocut current.
The surgeon performs a septotomy using a flexible endoscope, making incisions through the mucosal and submucosal layers to expose the cricopharyngeal bar.
The surgeon performs an endoscopic myotomy, cutting down to the base of the Zenker's pouch, allowing the endoscope to pass smoothly into the proximal esophagus.
The endoscopic myotomy creates a visible mucosal defect with mild bleeding observed at the site.
The surgeon uses a Conmet dura clip to close the septotomy defect in the upper esophagus, securing the tissue at the site of the incision.

This clip is easily rotatable with a one to one rotation even over the distal cap attachment and has an eleven millimeter jaw with a small tail making it very advantageous to use in tight positions such as this case.
It has a one step easy deployment with a tactile feedback to the assistant.
Following the initial placement of the clip, a small residual defect is visible in the surgical area.
The surgeon rotates a second duraclip in the tight space to successfully close the opposite side of the septotomy defect.
